executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented coordinated ransomware campaign with 103 organizations compromised in a single 48-hour period. High-profile victims include Canada Goose (consumer goods), National Money Mart (financial services with database leaked), Peterborough Public Health, Eastern Townships School Board, Municipality of North Perth, and MRC de Maskinongé district government. At least 20 distinct ransomware groups are actively targeting Canadian critical infrastructure, healthcare, education, and municipal services simultaneously. This represents a national-level cyber emergency requiring immediate coordinated response.

canada_landscape.summary: Canada is facing a critical ransomware crisis with 103 organizations compromised across all provinces in 48 hours. Public sector targets include Peterborough Public Health, Eastern Townships School Board, Municipality of North Perth, MRC de Maskinongé, and Workers Health & Safety Centre. High-value commercial targets include Canada Goose and National Money Mart (with confirmed database exfiltration).
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose compromised by Coinbase Cartel ransomware group. Active Green + Ross (automotive retail) compromised by Sinobi. Kids & Company (childcare services) compromised by Sinobi.
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company breached by Everest ransomware with database leaked. Morison Insurance Brokers compromised by Sinobi. Trucash financial services compromised by Lynx. Multiple accounting firms targeted: Pewarchuk CPA (Play), MG Chartered Professional Accountant (Qilin), Reidhurst Nagy (Incransom).

report_2w.executive_summary: CISA has added four actively exploited vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog, including CVE-2025-29635 (D-Link router RCE) and CVE-2025-48700 (Zimbra XSS), both being leveraged in the wild. Cisco ASA and FTD devices face three critical vulnerabilities (CVE-2025-20333, CVE-2025-20362, CVE-2025-20363) per CCCS advisory AL25-012, with threat actor UAT-4356 deploying persistent FIRESTARTER backdoor via chained exploits. ShinyHunters extortion group claims major breaches of ADT (10M records via vishing) and Udemy (1.4M records), while Citizens Bank faces lawsuits following an Everest ransomware incident. Canada saw unprecedented SMS blaster attacks causing 13M network disruptions and infiltrating tens of thousands of devices across Toronto streets.

canada_landscape.summary: Canada experienced a significant cyber-physical threat with SMS blasters in vehicles cruising Toronto streets, causing 13 million network disruptions, infiltrating tens of thousands of devices, and blocking emergency 911 calls in Project Lighthouse. The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued Alert AL25-012 urging immediate patching of three critical Cisco ASA and FTD vulnerabilities actively being exploited. German politicians faced Russian-attributed Signal phishing attacks, with Canadian officials potentially at risk given similar NATO/Five Eyes threat profiles.
canada_landscape.financial: Citizens Bank (US entity, not Canadian) confirmed third-party data breach with Everest ransomware involvement and class action lawsuits
